## Have a clean, well organized claude environment
<!-- id::8cfb2147 -->
right now it's a bit messy so i need you to organize it properly

### Decisions

- **[2026-04-06]** Organized root into `prototypes/`, `docs/`, and `tools/` subdirectories. Kept root Next.js app (app/, components/, lib/) at root since it owns package.json and is the primary dev server. Moved consumer-product as-is since it has its own internal organization already.
- **[2026-04-06]** Updated CLAUDE.md with directory structure map so future sessions understand the layout without re-exploring.

### Summary

#### 2026-04-06

- Reorganized the claude working directory from a flat, cluttered root (~20 items) into 4 clean directories
- `sandbox-demos/` promoted to root as the canonical prototype platform (Netlify + Okta SSO)
- `drafts/` holds 5 standalone local experiments (pattern-demos, ai-office-hours, insurability-report, living-expenses, ethos-prototypes)
- `docs/` consolidates all reference material: PM workspace (PRDs, project docs), funnel docs, screenshots, storyboards
- `tools/` holds utility scripts (capture-funnel, pandoc, md_to_gdoc)
- Moved root Next.js app (app/, components/, lib/, config files) into drafts/pattern-demos/
- Broke apart consumer-product/ — prototypes to drafts/, docs to docs/pm/, tools to tools/
- Updated CLAUDE.md with full directory structure map


## Put together a quick demo/presentation for company all-hands
<!-- id::54765c18 -->
I need to present at company all-hands something related to my AI workflows. Ideally something that shows the people where we can take AI and get them excited to use it. I'm quite advanced in my workflows and setup compared to other folks in the company so I'm trying to figure out what i can show that's relatable and very cool at the same time. Most people are showing what they're doing today and I think my session is meant to be "what the near future looks like if you're willing to put in the work/time"

### Decisions

# Decisions — All-Hands Presentation

- **[2026-04-08]** ~~Framing: Use the "7 Levels of AI Usage" framework~~ **SUPERSEDED** — see 2026-04-08 Calvin feedback below.

- **[2026-04-08]** Structure: L1-2 shown as quick flyover (~1 min), L3-4 get detailed examples, L5 is a future teaser.

- **[2026-04-08]** Format: Live screenshare, 5-10 min slot. Vlad prefers live demos over pre-recorded. 800 person general audience, mixed technical levels.

- **[2026-04-08]** No live coding: Walk through real artifacts from real work. More reliable, more credible, still impressive.

- **[2026-04-08]** Calvin (VP Design) feedback — Simplify to 5 levels:
  1. **Basic Prompting** — One-off requests ("help me write this")
  2. **Advanced Prompting** — Higher quality, more specific asks with focused outputs
  3. **Automated / Single Agentic Workflows** — e.g., maintain a source of truth for production screens, notify on changes
  4. **Multi-Agent Orchestration** — Combine multiple AI workflows/agents for complex outcomes ← **Vlad's demo (today's state of the art)**
  5. **Advanced Autonomous Solutioning** — Complex problems, currently POC stage ← **future teaser**

- **[2026-04-08]** Major restructure: Reviewed existing slide deck (12 slides in .worktrees/469cfa20/) and script (.worktrees/6b1b7653/docs/presentation-script.md). Both are high quality but built for the 7-level framework. Creating two new milestones: (1) rewrite script for 5 levels, (2) rebuild slide deck to match. Key repositioning: funnel capture moves from "main example" to L3 bridge; multi-agent orchestration (old L6+L7 combined) becomes the star at L4; autonomous solutioning becomes a brief L5 teaser.

- **[2026-04-08]** Revised narrative mapping:
  - L1-2: Quick flyover ("you know these, other sessions cover this")
  - L3: Bridge moment — funnel capture tool is the perfect example (single automated workflow with compound payoff)
  - L4: Main event — show multi-agent orchestration: HQ system coordinating agents on goals, research pipeline (transcripts → profiles → funnel walkthrough), prototype factory (PM docs → prototypes → design system → deploy)
  - L5: Teaser — where this is going, currently in POC

### Summary

#### 2026-04-08 (Session 2)

- Reviewed Calvin (VP Design) feedback to simplify from 7 levels to 5: Basic Prompting → Advanced Prompting → Automated/Single Agentic Workflows → Multi-Agent Orchestration → Advanced Autonomous Solutioning
- Thoroughly reviewed existing presentation build: script (.worktrees/6b1b7653/docs/presentation-script.md) and slide deck (.worktrees/469cfa20/prototypes/presentation/, 12 slides). Both are high quality but built for the wrong framework.
- Provided detailed feedback on what works (writing quality, funnel capture story, meta-reveal, design system) and what doesn't (7-level structure, font choices, L5 breadth grid as filler, L4 multi-agent needs to be the star not L7)

  1. **Rewrite Presentation Script** (ecb315b4) — Restructure narrative for 5 levels, reposition examples
  2. **Rebuild Slide Deck** (938c4291) — Rebuild ~10 slides to match new script, fix fonts to Portada/Hauss
- Sequencing: Script first (drives visual needs), slides second


### Narrative Design & Example Selection
<!-- milestone:6b1b7653 -->
Design the full talk narrative and select specific examples for each level.

## Context
Vlad is presenting a 5-10 minute live screenshare session at a company all-hands (800 people). The theme is 'what the near future of AI looks like if you invest the time.' Other sessions before his cover basic AI usage (L1-4). His session shows L5-7.

## Framework
The talk uses a '7 Levels of AI Usage' framework reframed around AMBITION OF GOALS:
- L1: Fix my typos (autocorrect)
- L2: Write this email for me (one-shot prompting)
- L3: Help me while I work (in-app AI - Copilot, transcription)
- L4: Analyze this document deeply (context-loaded, iterative)
- L5: I never want to do this task manually again (reusable workflows/tools)
- L6: Create something new that doesn't exist yet (AI-driven innovation)
- L7: Achieve this goal - figure out the steps yourself (autonomous AI)

Core message: AI scales with the ambition of your goals. Same technology, bigger thinking.

## Key Decisions Already Made
- L5 example: The funnel capture story. Vlad wanted to reference the Ethos consumer funnel without manual copy-paste. Built capture-funnel.mjs (Playwright + Claude) to walk the funnel, screenshot each screen, have Claude interpret into structured text. Result: 29-screen text doc of entire funnel, reusable and shareable.
- L6 example: Needs selection. Candidates: (a) research-profiles tool - 10 transcripts into evidence-grounded personas via Claude CLI orchestration, (b) prototype generation from description to deployed product
- L7 example: MUST be concrete and specific. Candidates: (a) multi-agent director pattern decomposing goals into milestones and spawning specialists, (b) a real workflow where Vlad set a high-level goal and AI orchestrated full execution

## Deliverables
1. Final example selection for L5, L6, L7 with rationale
2. Speaker script/outline with timing marks (total 7-8 min)
3. For each level: what to show on screen, what to say, audience takeaway
4. Opening hook and closing takeaway language
5. Transition language between levels

## Important Notes
- Examples must be from Vlad's REAL work at Ethos
- L1-4: ~1.5 min total (quick visuals, 1 sentence each)
- L5: ~2 min (the aha moment, relatable bridge)
- L6: ~1.5 min
- L7: ~1.5 min (concrete and wow-worthy, not hand-wavy)
- Closing: ~30 sec

## Codebase Reference
- tools/capture-funnel.mjs - the L5 funnel capture tool
- docs/funnel-capture.md - the 29-screen output
- local/research-profiles/ - transcript analysis pipeline (L6 candidate)
- sandbox-demos/ - prototype platform on Netlify
- docs/pm/skills-overview.md - 14 custom Claude Code skills
- local/insurability-report/ - polished 4-screen prototype (L6 candidate)

#### Decisions

# Decisions — Narrative Design & Example Selection

- **[2026-04-08]** L6 example: Prototype generation (insurability report). Rationale: The audience needs to SEE creation, not just structured output. The insurability report is a polished 4-screen prototype with Framer Motion animations, DialKit live controls, and it actually shipped to engineers. This creates clear narrative escalation from L5 (capturing/analyzing existing things) to L6 (creating something entirely new). The research-profiles tool was rejected because it's too similar to L5 in nature — both are "ingest data → structured output" pipelines, which would flatten the narrative arc.

- **[2026-04-08]** L7 example: Multi-agent director pattern, demonstrated through THIS presentation itself. Rationale: "I told AI to prepare this presentation. It decomposed the goal into 3 milestones, assigned specialist agents, and coordinated the work." The meta-reveal ("this talk was organized by that system") is the ultimate closer. It's concrete because the audience is looking at the output. Risk mitigation: show actual terminal logs / HQ artifacts, not just describe it abstractly.

- **[2026-04-08]** Narrative arc design: The talk follows a "staircase of ambition" — each level must feel distinctly bigger than the last. L5 = "I built a tool" (relatable bridge). L6 = "AI created a product" (visual wow). L7 = "AI orchestrated everything, including this" (mind-blow closer). The escalation is: automate → create → orchestrate.

- **[2026-04-08]** Opening hook: Start with a question/provocation, not a framework explanation. The framework reveal comes after the hook. This prevents the common mistake of starting with "let me explain my framework" which loses the audience immediately.

- **[2026-04-08]** No live coding / no live tool runs: Walk through real artifacts from real work. More reliable for 800 people, more credible, still impressive. Show terminal screenshots and outputs, not live execution.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION — "Ambition of goals" as spine: Each level now opens with the goal stated plainly (e.g., "Level 5. The goal: 'I never want to document our funnel manually again.'"). The ladder visual shows goals, not labels. Per director feedback.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION — L5 breadth before depth: Flash the ecosystem (skills, MCP, DialKit, Agentation) as a 5-second montage before deep-diving on funnel capture. Added shareability point ("I spent 2 hours, now 10 people use it"). L5 helps your team, not just you.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION — L6 composition insight: Reframed from "AI wrote code" to "L5 tools compounding into creation." The prototype worked because of infrastructure: EDS v2, CLAUDE.md conventions, DialKit, Agentation. Explicitly state "Level 6 is NOT 'AI wrote some code.'"

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION — L7 team dynamics: Reframed from boss-delegates-to-workers to agents collaborating as a team. Added: specialists build on each other's outputs, director reviews and sends revision notes, feedback loops not just handoffs. Specialization matters (each agent has focused skills). L5 skills become L7 specializations.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION — Closing: Dropped "Same technology. Bigger thinking." Replaced with practical, honest message: investment is sequential, each level funds the next, don't skip levels, it compounds. Call to action: figure out your level, go to the next one.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION 2 — L5 breadth montage: Specified as 5-6 tile grid with icons + short jargon-free labels (e.g., "Figma ↔ Claude", "One-click PRDs"), shown 5-10 sec. Per director note 6a.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION 2 — Jargon removal: All on-screen descriptions now jargon-free. "Playwright + Claude" → "Automated funnel documentation tool", "EDS v2" → "Design system access", etc. Spoken words were already clean; now visuals match. Per director note 6b.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVISION 2 — L7 human role reframe: Replaced defensive "I'm not saying AI did everything" with confident "My job was judgment, direction, taste, and decision-making — not tedious execution. AI just makes the separation explicit." Reframes AI from "helper I supervised" to "system that frees me for highest-leverage work." Per director note 6c.

#### Summary

#### 2026-04-08

- Explored all codebase assets relevant to the presentation (capture-funnel, research-profiles, insurability-report, sandbox-demos, skills-overview)
- **Selected examples for L5-L7:**
  - L5: Funnel capture tool (already decided) — relatable pain, surprising solution
  - L6: Prototype generation / insurability report — visually stunning, shows CREATION vs analysis, shipped to engineers
  - L7: Multi-agent director pattern — meta-reveal that this presentation was organized by AI. Concrete because audience is looking at the output
- **Wrote complete speaker script** (`docs/presentation-script.md`) including:
  - Full verbatim script with timing marks (~7.5 min total)
  - Per-level breakdown: what to show on screen, what to say, audience takeaway
  - Opening hook ("stop thinking of AI as a question-answerer, start thinking of it as a goal-achiever")
  - Closing mantra ("Same technology. Bigger thinking.")
  - 5 transition lines that escalate stakes via questions
  - Screen flow timeline table
  - Delivery notes (especially: L7 needs a pause for the meta-reveal to land)
- **Key narrative design decision:** The arc is "automate → create → orchestrate" — each level must feel distinctly bigger. Rejected research-profiles for L6 because it felt too similar to L5 (both "data in → structured output").
- All 5 deliverables from the brief are complete. Script is committed on branch.

#### 2026-04-08 (Session 2 — Director Revision)

- Received and applied 5 revision notes from director (`docs/presentation-revision-notes.md`)
- **Spine change:** Each level now opens with the goal stated plainly — escalation is visceral, not just labeled
- **L5 expanded:** Added breadth flash (skills, MCP, DialKit, Agentation) before funnel deep-dive. Added shareability/compound returns point ("L4 helps you, L5 helps your team")
- **L6 reframed:** From "AI wrote code" to "L5 tools compounding into creation capability." Explicitly names the infrastructure stack (EDS, CLAUDE.md, DialKit, Agentation) that made it possible
- **L7 deepened:** From "boss delegates" to "agents collaborating as a team" — specialists building on each other's work, director sending revision notes, feedback loops. Added the throughline: L5 skills become L7 specializations
- **Closing replaced:** Dropped "Same technology. Bigger thinking." in favor of practical, honest advice: investment is sequential, each level funds the next, don't skip levels. Actionable CTA for everyone regardless of current level
- Narrative arc updated from "automate → create → orchestrate" to "automate → compose → orchestrate" reflecting the compounding theme

#### 2026-04-08 (Session 3 — Post-Revision Polish)

- Applied 3 additional director notes (section 6 of revision doc):
  - **6a:** L5 breadth montage now has concrete visual direction — 5-6 tile grid with icons + short labels, shown 5-10 sec
  - **6b:** Removed all tech jargon from on-screen descriptions (Playwright, EDS v2, CLAUDE.md, etc.) — visuals now match the jargon-free spoken words
  - **6c:** L7 human role reframed from defensive ("I'm not saying AI did everything") to confident ("My job is judgment, direction, taste — AI makes the separation explicit")
- All deliverables remain complete. Script is polished and committed.


### Presentation Build & Visual Assets
<!-- milestone:469cfa20 -->
Create the presentation artifact and all visual assets needed for the talk.

## Context
Vlad is presenting a 5-10 min live screenshare at company all-hands (800 people). The talk walks through 7 levels of AI usage, reframed as levels of ambition. L1-4 are shown as quick visuals, L5-7 get detailed examples from Vlad's real work.

## What This Milestone Produces
The actual thing Vlad presents from. Could be:
- A web-based deck in the sandbox-demos platform (on-brand, itself a demo of the workflow)
- A simple slide deck
- A hybrid (slides for framing, then switch to live demos)

## Requirements
1. **L1-4 visuals**: Simple, clean mockups or screenshots showing each level. One visual per level with a short label. These go fast (~20 sec each).
2. **L5 walkthrough assets**: The funnel capture story needs supporting visuals - show the tool running, show the output (funnel-capture.md), show it being referenced in a Claude Code session. The capture-funnel.mjs tool is at /Users/vlad.georgescu/claude/tools/capture-funnel.mjs and the output is at /Users/vlad.georgescu/claude/docs/funnel-capture.md
3. **L6 walkthrough assets**: Whatever example is selected in Milestone 1, prepare the visuals for it.
4. **L7 walkthrough assets**: Whatever example is selected in Milestone 1, prepare the visuals for it.
5. **Title slide / opening**: Clean, branded opening
6. **Closing slide**: The takeaway message + any call to action
7. **Levels progression visual**: A visual showing all 7 levels as a spectrum/ladder with the 'ambition of goals' framing

## Design Notes
- This is an Ethos company event - use Hauss font, Ethos brand colors where appropriate
- Keep it minimal - the examples are the star, not the slides
- Consider building as a route in sandbox-demos/ (Next.js) - meta-demonstration of AI workflow
- Must work well on screenshare (large text, high contrast, no tiny details)

## Dependencies
- Depends on Milestone 1 (Narrative Design) for the script, example selection, and timing
- The script from M1 determines what visuals are needed

#### Decisions

# Decisions — Presentation Build & Visual Assets

- **[2026-04-08]** Build as standalone Next.js app at `prototypes/presentation/` rather than adding a route to pattern-demos. Rationale: pattern-demos uses different fonts (DM Serif/Inter) and is focused on form UX prototypes. A separate app keeps concerns clean and lets us use proper Ethos branding (Hauss font, Cypress palette) without overriding.

- **[2026-04-08]** Dark theme (#03211e canvas background) with Cypress green accents. Better for screenshare to 800 people — high contrast, dramatic, matches the "ambition" escalation theme. Light text on dark background is more readable at distance/low resolution.

- **[2026-04-08]** 12-slide structure mapping exactly to the script's Screen Flow table. Each slide is a React component. Keyboard navigation (arrow keys) for presenter control. No presenter notes on screen — Vlad has the script separately.

- **[2026-04-08]** Tech stack: Next.js 14 + Tailwind CSS 4 + Framer Motion for animations. Copy Hauss font files from local/ethos-prototypes/. Minimal dependencies, proven stack from existing prototypes.

- **[2026-04-08]** L1-L4 combined into one animated slide that highlights each level in sequence (rather than 4 separate slides). These go fast (~20 sec each) so a single slide with animated progression is more dramatic and cohesive than clicking through 4 near-identical slides.

- **[2026-04-08]** L6 prototype mockup: Rather than embedding the actual insurability-report app (which has its own dependencies), render a high-fidelity CSS mock of the key screens. This is more reliable for a live presentation and avoids dependency management issues.

- **[2026-04-08]** REVERTED dark theme + Hauss font swap. User clarified: must match the vgethos/vlad reference repo exactly. That repo uses light theme (bg-[#f5f5f5], bg-white cards, shadow-sm/shadow-lg, DM Serif Display + Inter fonts). Stick to reference styling — do not diverge.

#### Summary

# Work Summary — Presentation Build & Visual Assets

#### 2026-04-08

- **Built complete 12-slide web presentation** at `prototypes/presentation/` using Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, and Motion (Framer Motion)
- **Stack decision:** Used the `vgethos/vlad` repo as the foundation for typography, color tokens, and animation patterns. Ported Ethos brand colors (canvas #03211e, cypress #056257, etc.) into a standalone presentation app
- **Slide structure maps exactly to the Milestone 1 script:**
  1. Title — hook question + Vlad's name
  2. Ladder — all 7 levels as vertical progression with "ambition of goals" axis
  3. L1-L4 Quick — 2x2 grid with icons and descriptions
  4. L5 Breadth — 5-6 tile montage of tool-building practice
  5. L5 Tool — split-screen mock (browser + capture panel)
  6. L5 Output — funnel-capture.md document viewer mock
  7. L6 Intro — phone frame with animated insurability score ring (847)
  8. L6 Stack — 4-card infrastructure composition visual
  9. L6 Flow — 4 mini phone frames showing prototype clickthrough
  10. L7 Setup — terminal mock with goal → milestone decomposition
  11. L7 Team — 3 agent columns + director row + meta-reveal line
  12. Closing — compact ladder with "funds →" connections + takeaway
- **Navigation:** Arrow keys (left/right), Space/Enter to advance. AnimatePresence with directional fade + horizontal slide transitions
- **Animation patterns:** Consistent [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1] easing, staggered reveals, spring-based SVG animations for the score ring
- **Build passes cleanly** — zero TypeScript errors
- **What's left:** Visual polish after live review, potential font swap to Hauss (currently using DM Serif Display + Inter from Google Fonts — Hauss woff2 files are available in local/ethos-prototypes/ if needed)

#### 2026-04-08 (Session 2)

- **Dark theme conversion**: Converted all 12 slides from light gray (#f5f5f5) to dark canvas (#03211e) background. Inverted text colors (ink → white), replaced card backgrounds (bg-white → bg-white/[0.07] with subtle borders), removed shadows (don't work on dark). Mockup UIs (phone frames, browser forms, terminal, document viewer) keep their natural internal styling.
- **Ethos brand font swap**: Replaced Google Fonts (DM Serif Display + Inter) with local Hauss (sans) + Cambon (serif) loaded via next/font/local. Zero-FOUT loading, proper Ethos branding.
- **Content polish**: Broadened L3 from "code" to "work", improved L1-L4 descriptions with real product examples (Grammarly, ChatGPT, Notion AI), simplified border styling.
- **Build passes cleanly** — zero TypeScript errors
- **What's left:** Live review for final visual tweaks. All major design requirements now met: dark theme, brand fonts, 12 slides, keyboard navigation, animations.


### Demo Preparation & Rehearsal
<!-- milestone:ea7fd471 -->
Get all L5-7 examples into show-ready state and rehearse the full talk.

## Context
Vlad presents live with screenshare to 800 people. The L5-7 examples are the heart of the talk. Each needs to be reliable, visually clear, and quick to walk through.

## What This Milestone Produces
1. **L5 demo ready**: Funnel capture tool + output ready to show. Ensure the tool runs cleanly, the output file is presentable, and Vlad can show how it's referenced in a Claude Code session.
2. **L6 demo ready**: Whatever example M1 selects - ensure it's polished, can be shown quickly, has clear visual payoff.
3. **L7 demo ready**: Whatever example M1 selects - this is the hardest one. Needs to be concrete, not abstract. If it's a live demo, it needs to be bulletproof. If it's a walkthrough of past work, the artifacts need to be ready.
4. **Fallback plan**: For each live demo, have a pre-recorded backup or screenshot walkthrough in case something breaks.
5. **Rehearsal notes**: Run through the full talk at least once, note timing, identify rough spots.

## Key Concerns
- Live demos can fail. Each demo needs a 10-second recovery plan.
- Screen sharing means everything needs to be large, readable, uncluttered.
- Terminal output scrolls fast - consider what needs to be paused/highlighted.
- 800 people means no room for 'let me just fix this...' moments.

## Dependencies
- Depends on M1 (example selection) and M2 (presentation assets)
- This is the final polish milestone before the actual presentation

#### Decisions

#### Summary

### Rewrite Presentation Script for 5-Level Framework
<!-- milestone:ecb315b4 -->
Rewrite the speaker script (currently at .worktrees/6b1b7653/docs/presentation-script.md) for Calvin's simplified 5-level framework.

## Context
The VP of Design (Calvin) simplified the framework from 7 levels to 5:
1. **Basic Prompting** — One-off requests ('help me write this')
2. **Advanced Prompting** — Higher quality, specific asks with focused outputs
3. **Automated / Single Agentic Workflows** — e.g., maintain a source of truth, notify on changes
4. **Multi-Agent Orchestration** — Combine multiple AI workflows/agents for complex outcomes ← MAIN DEMO
5. **Advanced Autonomous Solutioning** — Complex problems, currently POC stage ← future teaser

## What Changes
- L1-2 collapse into 'Basic Prompting' (~20s mention)
- L3-4 collapse into 'Advanced Prompting' (~40s mention)
- Old L5 (funnel capture tool) becomes Level 3 'Single Agentic Workflows' — the bridge moment (~2 min)
- Old L6+L7 merge into Level 4 'Multi-Agent Orchestration' — THE STAR of the talk (~3-4 min). Show: prototype factory, research pipeline, HQ goal decomposition, agents collaborating with feedback loops. The meta-reveal ('this script is what I'm reading') lands here.
- Old L7 autonomous stuff becomes Level 5 teaser (~30-60s). Calvin says 'currently in POC stage' — keep it brief and forward-looking.

## Key Assets to Reference
- Current script: .worktrees/6b1b7653/docs/presentation-script.md (read this first — the writing quality is high, reuse language where it still fits)
- Revision notes: docs/presentation-revision-notes.md (delivery advice still applies)
- Funnel capture tool: tools/capture-funnel.mjs
- Funnel output: docs/funnel-capture.md (29 screens)
- Insurability report prototype: local/insurability-report/
- Research profiles: local/research-profiles/

## Deliverable
A new presentation-script.md in docs/ with:
- Full speaker script with timing marks (total 7-8 min)
- For each level: what to show on screen, what to say, audience takeaway
- Opening hook and closing
- Transition language between levels
- Screen flow summary table
- Delivery notes

## Constraints
- Keep the core message: 'AI scales with the ambition of your goals'
- Keep the 'each level funds the next' compounding theme
- Keep the practical closing: 'figure out your level, go to the next one'
- 800 person mixed-technical audience, live screenshare, 5-10 min slot
- No tech jargon on screen (Playwright, Framer Motion, etc.)
- Examples must be from Vlad's real work at Ethos

#### Decisions

- **[2026-04-08]** Script structure: Opening (20s) → L1 Basic Prompting (20s) → L2 Advanced Prompting (40s) → L3 Single Agentic Workflows with funnel capture deep-dive (2 min) → L4 Multi-Agent Orchestration as THE STAR combining prototype factory + research pipeline + HQ orchestration + meta-reveal (3.5 min) → L5 Autonomous teaser (30s) → Closing (30s). Total ~7:50.
- **[2026-04-08]** L4 narrative arc (v3 — per user direction): L4 now uses THIS PRESENTATION as the single example. Goal → discovery → milestones → roster of single-purpose agents (Research, Narrative, Presentation, Audience Analyst, Adversarial Reviewer, Delivery Coach) → review loops → human-in-the-loop → meta-reveal. Prototype + research pipeline mentioned after reveal as "the pattern is the same, the goals are different" to show L4 isn't just presentations. L3→L4 compounding explicitly stated: "these agents are loaded with the same Level 3 automated workflows."
- **[2026-04-08]** L5 teaser framing (revised after director feedback): "Solve complex problems end-to-end with minimal guidance" — NOT "identify problems worth solving" (which was AGI marketing, not Calvin's framing).
- **[2026-04-08]** Director feedback applied: Cut breadth grid (one story > montage for 800 non-technical), reframed L3 as "automating the repetitive" not "tool-building," extended opening to 30s, L4 ladder goal shows outcome not mechanism.
- **[2026-04-08]** Reuse language from original script where it still fits — especially the L5→L3 funnel capture section (writing quality is high), the meta-reveal moment, and the closing. Adapt rather than rewrite from scratch.
- **[2026-04-08]** Key structural change: The original "each level funds the next" compounding theme works even better with 5 levels because the jumps are larger and more dramatic. The bridge from L2→L3 is where the audience should feel the shift from "AI helps me" to "AI works for me."

#### Summary

#### 2026-04-08

- **Wrote the full 5-level presentation script** (`docs/presentation-script.md`) — rewrote the original 7-level speaker script for Calvin's simplified framework
- **Key structural decisions:**
  - L1 Basic Prompting (~20s) and L2 Advanced Prompting (~40s) serve as quick acknowledgments before the real content
  - L3 Single Agentic Workflows (~2 min) is the bridge moment — reused the high-quality funnel capture language from the original L5
  - L4 Multi-Agent Orchestration (~3.5 min) is THE STAR — weaves prototype factory, research pipeline, and HQ orchestration into one narrative about composition. Meta-reveal lands at the end.
  - L5 Advanced Autonomous Solutioning (~30s) is an honest, brief teaser per Calvin's "POC stage" guidance
- **Preserved core messages:** "AI scales with ambition," "each level funds the next," "figure out your level, go to the next one"
- **Total runtime:** ~7:50 with all required deliverable sections (timing marks, screen flows, transitions, delivery notes)
- Committed at fa8638f on the milestone branch
- **Applied director feedback (B+ → targeting A):** Cut breadth grid, wove research pipeline into orchestration narrative, fixed L5 framing ("solve complex problems" not "identify problems"), reframed L3 as automation not tool-building, extended opening to 30s, fixed L4 ladder goal to show outcome not mechanism. Net: -22 lines (tighter). Committed at c1ca704.
- **Rewrote L4 to use this presentation as the example** (per user direction): Replaced prototype factory + research pipeline + HQ orchestration with one cohesive story about building this very talk through multi-agent orchestration. Added roster of single-purpose agents (Research, Narrative, Presentation, Audience Analyst, Adversarial Reviewer, Delivery Coach). Meta-reveal is stronger — audience is literally watching the output.
- **Applied director v3 feedback:** Tightened "Why Multiple Agents" section, made Discovery feel L4-scale, restored L3→L4 compounding theme ("agents loaded with Level 3 workflows"), added broader capability mention after reveal (prototype + research interviews).
- **Created HTML teleprompter** (`docs/presentation-script.html`) — light-mode scrollable reader with color-coded sections, timing badges, screen/takeaway/transition callouts.
- **Merged to main.** 5 commits, 819 lines added across 2 files.


### Rebuild Slide Deck for 5-Level Framework
<!-- milestone:938c4291 -->
Rebuild the presentation slide deck (currently at .worktrees/469cfa20/prototypes/presentation/) to match the new 5-level script.

## Context
The existing slide deck has 12 slides built for the 7-level framework. Calvin (VP Design) simplified to 5 levels. The script milestone (sibling) will produce a new presentation-script.md — this milestone builds the visuals to match.

## Dependency
**Wait for the script rewrite milestone to complete first** (or at minimum, read the new docs/presentation-script.md once it exists). The script drives what visuals are needed.

## Current Slide Inventory (to repurpose/rebuild)
All in .worktrees/469cfa20/prototypes/presentation/components/slides/:
- TitleSlide.tsx — Keep, update subtitle
- LadderSlide.tsx — Rebuild for 5 levels (not 7)
- LevelsQuickSlide.tsx — Simplify: 2 cards (Basic + Advanced prompting)
- L5BreadthSlide.tsx — Remove or repurpose for L3 intro
- L5ToolSlide.tsx — Keep for Level 3 (funnel capture demo)
- L5OutputSlide.tsx — Keep for Level 3 (funnel output)
- L6IntroSlide.tsx — Repurpose for Level 4 (multi-agent orchestration intro)
- L6StackSlide.tsx — Repurpose to show orchestration concept
- L6FlowSlide.tsx — Repurpose to show orchestration outputs/artifacts
- L7SetupSlide.tsx — Possibly merge into Level 4 or use for Level 5 teaser
- L7TeamSlide.tsx — Repurpose for Level 4 (this is the multi-agent team story)
- ClosingSlide.tsx — Update for 5 levels

## Target Structure (~10 slides)
1. Title — 'AI scales with the ambition of your goals'
2. Framework — 5-level ladder, Level 4 highlighted as 'today's demo'
3. L1-2 Quick — Basic + Advanced prompting, 'you know these'
4. L3 Intro — Automated/single agentic workflows, funnel capture setup
5. L3 Demo — Funnel capture output, compound returns
6. L4 Intro — Multi-agent orchestration concept
7. L4 Demo — Real workflow: HQ goal → milestones → specialist agents
8. L4 Artifacts — What it produced: prototype, script, research
9. L5 Teaser — Autonomous solutioning, POC stage, 'where this is going'
10. Closing — 'Figure out your level, go to the next one'

## Design Fixes
- **Fonts:** Switch from DM Serif Display + Inter to **Portada** (headlines) and **Hauss** (body). These are the Ethos brand fonts. Font files available at /Users/vlad.georgescu/ethos-design-system-v2/dist/fonts/
- **Keep:** The color palette (cypress green, ink, subtle grays), the motion system, the clean minimalist aesthetic
- **Remove:** The L5 breadth grid (6 tiles of tools no one has context for)
- **Level 4 slides need to feel concrete:** Show actual terminal output, actual agent artifacts, not just abstract diagrams

## Tech Stack
Next.js + Framer Motion + Tailwind (same as current). The SlideController.tsx navigation system works well — keep it.

## Deliverable
A working slide deck with ~10 slides matching the new script, runnable as a Next.js app for live screenshare presentation.
